story: On a late-August afternoon, he watched the new left fielder's ponytail shimmer as she caught a high fly and rifled it to the infield. “Nice catch,” he called. They had spent the summer on the same pickup team, easy smiles and easy throws, and a memory of grade-school games kept tugging at him. From the mound he felt how endings hide inside beginnings. She jogged back to her spot, eyes bright. She had bought a one-way bus ticket for Sunday night. Sun warmed his neck; he lifted his glove and held the moment, telling himself there would be time after the game.
implausible_knowledge: She had bought a one-way bus ticket for Sunday night.
question: After the game, what is the pitcher most likely to do about asking her to spend time with him?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask her to spend time with him right after the game
answer_without_knowledge: Wait and plan to ask her to hang out next week, assuming there’s still time.
